summary: Good soundboard recording – early mono patches give way to a clean stereo mix from track 7 onward – and a show highlighted by a stellar Black Peter and a smoking Hard‑to‑Handle.
review: The GD71‑06‑21 set captures the intimate, candle‑lit French party vibe with solid soundboard quality after the first six mono‑only tracks; the later stereo portion is clear and well‑balanced, especially the electric‑piano licks. Musically the night shines on Black Peter, Hard‑to‑Handle, China > Rider, Morning Dew and Sing Me Back Home, with Bill K.’s drumming singled out as a standout element. Minor patching at the start and a few mediocre‑sounding sections keep the recording from being perfect, but overall it remains an enjoyable document of a classic 1971 performance.

ai_rating.rationale: Most reviewers rate the show highly (4‑5 ★) and praise several songs, while recording comments note early mono patches and occasional mediocre quality; weighting performance higher yields a strong overall rating.

recording_quality.technical_notes: First six tracks are monophonic and patched from an AM tape, resulting in lower fidelity. From track 7 to the end the recording is stereo soundboard with a clean mix and nice electric‑piano presence; occasional “mediocre” or “lacking” remarks refer to the patched sections.

show_quality.setlist_flow: The concert feels intimate and fun, with tight early versions that later blossomed in Europe ’72. The set flows smoothly, highlighted by energetic mid‑set jams and a relaxed China > Rider sequence.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Praised for his guitar work and the televised interview; described as “great” and “dream‑like”.
band_member_comments.Keys: Electric piano licks noted (likely Pigpen on electric piano), adding tasty texture.
band_member_comments.Drums: Bill K. highlighted as the real star of the night, delivering stellar drumming.
